categories.title: Recent Fixes and Improvements in ElizaOS

content.text: The ElizaOS team resolved a documentation issue related to CLI plugin commands. Previously, users encountered 'subcommand not found' errors due to incorrect syntax in the documentation for `elizaos plugin <subcommand>`. The updated documentation now reflects the correct usage, improving clarity and reducing user confusion. This change is low-risk as it only affects documentation and does not impact runtime behavior.

A bug causing port conflicts during local development was fixed. When running `npx elizaos start`, users previously encountered an 'agents already exists' error if the default port was in use. The updated behavior now checks for port availability and automatically selects the next available port, ensuring smoother project startup. This fix is low-risk and does not require documentation updates.

A bug in the `normalizeJsonString` function was fixed to correctly handle nested JSON objects. The previous implementation added extra quotation marks around nested objects, resulting in invalid JSON. The updated regular expression now avoids this issue, and new test cases have been added to ensure proper behavior. This is a low-risk change with no documentation updates required.

The non-functional 'Delete Character' button in the agent settings UI has been fixed. The issue stemmed from a database foreign key constraint that caused timeouts. The fix includes improved error handling, UI feedback, and timeout mechanisms to prevent the interface from hanging. This medium-risk change affects both backend deletion logic and frontend behavior, but does not require documentation updates.

As of the latest data, Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) is trading at $78,142.47, making it the highest-valued asset among the listed cryptocurrencies. Wrapped Ethereum (WETH) follows with a current price of $1,579.21. Solana (SOL) is priced at $105.84, while ai16z, a lower-cap token, is trading at $0.1086.

The Local-AI plugin underwent maintenance updates to improve modularity and remove outdated dependencies. Specifically, the fastembed and node-llama-cpp components were externalized to streamline the plugin's architecture and facilitate better integration and maintenance.
Additionally, all remaining references to 'ollama' were removed from the Local-AI plugin. This cleanup ensures consistency and prevents confusion with deprecated or unrelated components.

categories.title: ElizaOS Development Updates: Plugin Fixes, UI Enhancements, and Bug Resolutions

content.text: A documentation issue related to the ElizaOS CLI plugin commands (`add`, `remove`, `list`) was resolved. The previous documentation incorrectly referenced `elizaos plugin <subcommand>`, which led to 'subcommand not found' errors. The updated documentation now reflects the correct syntax, improving usability and reducing confusion for users.

A bug causing port conflicts when running `npx elizaos start` was fixed. Previously, if the default port was in use, the system would throw an 'agents already exists' error. The updated logic now checks for port availability and automatically selects the next available port, improving the local development experience.

The `normalizeJsonString` function was updated to fix a bug that incorrectly added extra quotation marks to nested JSON objects. The fix involved refining the regular expression used for normalization. New test cases were added to ensure the function behaves correctly with nested structures.

The non-functional 'Delete Character' button in the agent settings UI was fixed. The issue stemmed from database foreign key constraints causing timeouts. The fix included backend changes to the deletion logic, improved error handling, and UI feedback enhancements. The update ensures smoother deletion of agents, even those with active logs or multiple rooms.

UI components received a visual update to improve form element visibility. Input fields, text areas, and select elements now feature darker borders (gray-600) and enhanced focus states with thicker rings. These changes improve accessibility and visual consistency across the application.

Several internal improvements were made to the plugin system. These include enhancements to the plugin installation strategy, removal of outdated references to 'ollama' in the local-AI plugin, and externalization of dependencies like `fastembed` and `node-llama-cpp`. These changes streamline plugin management and improve maintainability.
